title: loa-status.sh: strict arg parsing + did-you-mean + NO_COLOR/TTY color guard
evidence: probe: --jsno → full human output, exit 0 (silent unknown-flag fallthrough)
probe: piped output contains 8 ANSI escape lines with and without NO_COLOR=1
loa-status.sh:30-35 unguarded color vars; lib/dx-utils.sh:50 has the correct in-house guard
0 test files reference loa-status
diff_sketch: Reject unknown flags with exit 2 + usage + nearest-match hint; gate RED/GREEN/.../NC block on [[ -z ${NO_COLOR:-} && -t 1 ]]; bats tests for --jsno rejection, NO_COLOR, piped-ANSI-free.
risk: low — additive strictness; JSON mode unchanged

title: block-destructive FR-2: allow bounded hidden-dir ./.name/ (except ./.git) + truthful block messages
evidence: live incident 2026-07-10: message said "Use './path/' explicit form", then blocked ./.loa/qmd/
block-destructive-bash.sh:847 _re_allow_list '\./[^/*.]' rejects all hidden dirs
block-destructive-bash.sh:1029-1031 both messages
diff_sketch: Extend allow to ./.<alnum>… while keeping ./., ./.., glob chars, and ./.git* blocked (explicit guard, no lookahead); rewrite both messages to name the forms that actually pass + trash + find -delete alternatives; bats: ./.loa-tmp/ allowed, ./.git/ still blocked, ./.. still blocked, message text pinned.
risk: security-sensitive — Fable-authored; never weakens: ./.git explicitly retained as blocked

title: beads-health.sh: --help support + content-aware jsonl staleness (kills permanent DEGRADED loop)
evidence: probe bh-3: --help → 'Unknown option: --help' exit 1
beads-health.sh:305-315 mtime-age staleness; recommendation 'run: br sync' cannot clear it (sync is hash-based, mtime untouched) — observed live 2026-07-11
diff_sketch: Add --help/-h/help printing usage+exit-code dictionary; when age>threshold, verify content currency (br export to temp + hash compare, or .last-sync-verified marker) before flagging stale; unknown-flag error echoes usage.
risk: low — health check only becomes more truthful

title: workflow-state.sh: scope completed_sprints to the current sprint plan
evidence: live JSON: total_sprints:1, completed_sprints:100, progress_percent:95, state:complete
workflow-state.sh:70-77 counts current plan headings; :79-91 counts ALL historical a2a/sprint-* dirs
diff_sketch: Count completed only among sprint IDs present in current sprint.md (or clamp to total); bats test with synthetic a2a history.

title: classify-merge-pr.sh: recognize release/* branch merges + release(...) subjects as cycle-type
evidence: production incident 2026-07-10: PR #1201 (release/v1.196.0-mechanical-floor) classified non-cycle → auto-tagged v1.195.1 patch; manual delete+retag required
memory: feedback_named_release_pattern.md
diff_sketch: Merge-subject regex gains 'from <owner>/release/' and conventional 'release(...)' recognition → cycle; bats table test incl. the real #1201 subject.
risk: low — post-merge pipeline consumes pr_type; cycle path is the richer one

title: NEW loa-capabilities.sh --json: machine-readable contract surface for the agent-facing script family
evidence: S-3: no capabilities surface exists; agents discover contracts by reading source (373 scripts)
skill Axiom 9
diff_sketch: Small read-only script emitting {version (from .loa-version.json), generated_from, scripts: [{path, purpose, json_mode, help, exit_codes}], env_vars, exit_code_conventions} for the ~20 core agent-facing scripts; static curated table (drift-checked by a bats test that verifies listed scripts exist + respond to --help as declared).

title: Mega-command: loa-status.sh --triage [--json] composing status + doctor-quick + next-step
evidence: loa-status --json and loa-doctor --json exist separately; no single-call triage (3 round-trips → 1)
skill Axiom 10 / Σ operator
diff_sketch: --triage runs existing status JSON + loa-doctor --quick --json + golden_suggest_command, merges into one envelope {status, health, next, capabilities_hint}; human mode prints compact triage. No new logic — composition only.
risk: low — additive flag reusing existing outputs

title: dx-utils dx_unknown_flag helper (usage echo + Levenshtein-1 did-you-mean) wired into 5 strict-rejecting scripts
evidence: 17/40 intent probes → useless_error; all bare 'Unknown option: X' with no usage, no suggestion
intent results bh-1..5, sb-1..5, vsc-1..5, cr-5
diff_sketch: Add dx_unknown_flag(bad, valid...) to lib/dx-utils.sh: prints Unknown option + nearest valid flag (edit distance ≤2) + one-line usage; wire into the 5 scripts' unknown-arg branches; bats: --jsno → mentions --json.
risk: low — error paths only, exit codes preserved

title: memory-query.sh: guard shift-crash (zero-output failure) + value-required errors
evidence: intent probe mq-4: crashes on shift 2 with 1 arg under set -e — exit 1, both streams EMPTY (worst-case silent crash)
diff_sketch: Replace bare 'shift 2' with value-presence check → 'Error: <flag> requires a value' + usage + exit 2; bats test.

title: validate-skill-capabilities.sh: fix unbound-$2 leak on --skill (raw bash error) + value-required message
evidence: intent probe vsc-3: 'line 36: $2: unbound variable' raw bash internal error, exit 1
diff_sketch: Guard ${2:-} with explicit 'Error: --skill requires a skill name' + usage + exit 2; bats test.

title: NO_COLOR/TTY color guard for the remaining unguarded-ANSI agent-facing scripts
evidence: sweep: ansi_refs>0 guard_refs=0 for all five; F6 scorer: validate-skill-capabilities lines 312-315 unconditional ANSI
dx-utils.sh:44-60 is the in-house correct pattern
diff_sketch: Gate each script's color-var block on [[ -z ${NO_COLOR:-} && -t 1 ]] (same shape as dx-utils); shared bats test looping the five scripts piping output and asserting zero \033 bytes.

title: semver-bump.sh: accept 'help'/-h, echo usage on unknown args, document exit dictionary in --help
evidence: intent probes sb-1..5: bare 'ERROR: Unknown argument: X' exit 2, no usage echo
exit dictionary (0/1/2) lives only in header comment, not in --help output
diff_sketch: Unknown-arg branch echoes usage; add exit-code dictionary lines to usage(); accept 'help' and '-h'; bats test.

title: golden-path.sh: direct-execution guard — usage on stderr + exit 2 instead of silent no-op exit 0
evidence: F1 inventory: bash golden-path.sh --help/-h/help/<bare> ALL exit 0 with zero output (verified live); header says 'source me' only in a comment
diff_sketch: At EOF: if [[ ${BASH_SOURCE[0]} == "$0" ]] print 'this is a sourced library; usage: source …; functions: …' to stderr, exit 2; bats: direct exec → exit 2 + message; sourcing still exit 0.
risk: low — no caller executes it directly (verified: all callers source)
